evals.expected_output: A complete seq command implementation in commands/seq.go with all flags (-w, -s, -f), registration in registry.go, integration tests, and fuzz tests. Should handle seq LAST, seq FIRST LAST, and seq FIRST INCREMENT LAST forms.
expectations: Creates a commands/seq.go file with a Seq struct implementing the Command interface
Includes var _ Command = (*Seq)(nil) interface compliance check
Registers NewSeq() in DefaultRegistry() in registry.go
Implements -w flag for equal-width zero-padded output
Implements -s flag for custom separator string
Implements -f flag for printf-style format string
Handles all three argument forms: seq LAST, seq FIRST LAST, seq FIRST INCR LAST
Handles floating-point increments correctly
Writes integration tests covering each flag
Writes fuzz tests using assertSecureFuzzOutcome

evals.expected_output: A complete fold command implementation in commands/fold.go with all flags (-w, -s, -b), registration in registry.go, integration tests, and fuzz tests. Should wrap lines at specified width, with byte-counting and word-breaking options.
expectations: Creates a commands/fold.go file with a Fold struct implementing the Command interface
Includes var _ Command = (*Fold)(nil) interface compliance check
Registers NewFold() in DefaultRegistry() in registry.go
Implements -w flag for custom line width (default 80)
Implements -s flag for breaking at word boundaries (spaces)
Implements -b flag for counting bytes instead of columns
Reads from stdin when no file arguments given
Writes integration tests covering each flag
Writes fuzz tests using assertSecureFuzzOutcome

evals.prompt: Add `strings` from coreutils — I need it for inspecting binary files
evals.expected_output: A complete strings command implementation in commands/strings.go with all flags (-n, -t, -a, -e), registration in registry.go, integration tests, and fuzz tests. Should extract printable strings from binary data.
expectations: Creates a commands/strings.go file with a Strings struct implementing the Command interface
Includes var _ Command = (*Strings)(nil) interface compliance check
Registers NewStrings() in DefaultRegistry() in registry.go
Implements -n flag for minimum string length (default 4)
Implements -t flag for offset format (d/o/x)
Implements -a flag for scanning the whole file
Implements -e flag for character encoding selection
Reads from stdin when no file arguments given
Writes integration tests covering each flag
Writes fuzz tests using assertSecureFuzzOutcome
